**Why I'm being careful here:**

That kind of hallucinated fingerprint data would actively undermine your research.

**What I'd suggest for your paper:**
If you're studying model fingerprinting seriously, treat self-reported model identities as **low-trust signals**. The reliable methods are: (1) the model's system card / technical report from the provider, (2) probing benchmarks, (3) logprob/perplexity signatures, and (4) the actual weight releases for open models.
